comparators.limitation: Not an execution runtime, graph database, vector store, or replacement for existing project-specific agent instructions.
comparators.name: Owledge
comparators.positioning: Best when long-running projects need durable plans, evidence, reviews, handoffs, decisions, and proof artifacts across agents.

comparators.limitation: Usually not a project-wide reviewed memory contract or cross-project proof layer.
comparators.name: Superpowers-style execution frameworks
comparators.positioning: Strong for coding workflow discipline, implementation flow, and agent execution structure.

comparators.limitation: A graph view should consume reviewed memory; it should not become the canonical truth by itself.
comparators.name: Graphify-style graph visualizers
comparators.positioning: Strong for seeing relationships, dependencies, and clusters once clean source records exist.

comparators.limitation: Needs explicit contracts, review status, and scoped context generation before agents can consume it reliably.
comparators.name: LLM Wiki / Obsidian-style knowledgebases
comparators.positioning: Strong for human knowledge capture, linking, and existing taxonomy.

comparators.limitation: Runtime memory and logs are not the same as reviewed project memory with reusable decisions and lessons.
comparators.name: Claude Code / Codex-style agent runtimes
comparators.positioning: Strong for executing tasks, editing files, running tests, and operating agent sessions.

comparators.limitation: Often depends on implicit team conventions unless paired with a stable memory and evidence contract.
comparators.name: Custom harnesses and hooks
comparators.positioning: Strong for highly tailored orchestration and local automation.

interpretation: Owledge is strongest as a durable memory, evidence, context hygiene, and traceability layer beside execution runtimes, graph views, wikis, and harnesses.
scoring_note: Scores describe fit for Owledge's memory/proof positioning, not absolute product quality.
